name: Suspicious Macro-Enabled Document
description: Employee received an Office or PDF document that prompted them to enable macros, enable editing, or disable security warnings.
title: Report a Document That Asked You to Enable Macros
intro:
Documents that ask you to **Enable Content**, **Enable Editing**, or **Enable Macros** to view them are one of the most common ways attackers get in. You did the right thing pausing.

Upload the file if you can and tell us where it came from. **Please do not click 'Enable Content' or run any macros** while we look at it.
submit_message: Thanks for flagging this before clicking through. We'll analyze the document and email you back with what to do next.

fields.label: The document
fields.help: Upload the file if you can. Don't open it again to grab it, just upload from where it was saved.

fields.label: Document filename
fields.help: Include the extension if you can see it (.docm, .xlsm, .pdf, etc.).

fields.label: What type of document is it?
Where did the document come from?
options: Email attachment from outside the company
Email attachment from someone inside the company
fields.sample_value: Email attachment from outside the company

fields.label: What did it ask you to do?
options: Click a link inside the document
Type a password or other info

fields.label: Did you enable macros or editing?
options: I enabled editing but not macros

fields.label: Anything else that seemed off?
fields.help: Was the sender unexpected? Did the document look blurry or like a fake preview? Did it claim to be from a specific service?
fields.sample_value: Email said it was a purchase order from a vendor I've never worked with. The document showed a blurry image saying 'This document is protected. Enable content to view.'

fields.label: Device the document is on
fields.help: Hostname or asset tag if you know it.

alert_template.title: Macro-enabled doc reported by {{reporter_email}}: {{document_name}}
